items.title: Tigrayan forces preparing offensive against federal gov, Addis Ababa warns
items.description: Fears are mounting in Ethiopia over reports that forces in the northern Tigray region are preparing an offensive against the federal government in the coming days.

items.title: Hostilities must stop in Lebanon too says Meloni
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 15 - In a statement welcoming the deal reached between Washington and Tehran to stop the Iran war, Premier Giorgia Meloni said Monday that "hostilities must also cease in Lebanon". Meloni said that "Italy will continue working to support Lebanese sovereignty". (see related). (ANSA). Read article...

items.title: Israel must stop bombing Beirut says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - LUXEMBOURG, JUN 15 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Monday that Israel must stop bombing Beirut after the United States and Iran reached an agreement to end the war in the Middle East. "We must ensure that the fighting in Lebanon stops too, that Israel no longer bombs Beirut," Tajani said on the sidelines of an EU foreign affairs council in Luxembourg. In her statement welcoming the deal reached between Washington and Tehran, Premier Giorgia Meloni said that "hostilities must also cease in Lebanon". Meloni said that "Italy will continue working to support Leba

items.title: US-Iran deal is 'opportunity for peace that must be seized' says Meloni
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 15 - Premier Giorgia Meloni on Monday welcomed the agreement reached between Washington and Tehran to end the Iran war. "Last night, together with France, Germany, and the United Kingdom, we had already expressed our strong appreciation for the memorandum of understanding signed by the United States and Iran hours ago," Meloni said in a statement, referring to a joint declaration she signed with the leaders of the three other European powers. "Heartfelt thanks go to all the mediators, and in particular to Qatar and Pakistan, who made this agreement possible. "This is an oppo

items.title: Italy willing to take part in Strait of Hormuz naval mission
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 15 - Italy is willing to take part in an international naval mission for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after Washington and Tehran reached an agreement to end the Iran war, Premier Giorgia Meloni said on Monday. "The principles are clear: Iran cannot acquire a nuclear weapon and freedom of navigation must be guaranteed," Meloni said in a statement. "We stand ready, together with our partners and without prejudice to the necessary authorisation from Parliament, to contribute to an international naval presence to support the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz". items.title: Kenya: All Eyes On EPRA As Ruto's Sh10 Diesel Cut Pledge Faces Test Amid US-Iran Deal Hopes
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Motorists, transporters and businesses are awaiting Sunday's fuel price review with heightened anticipation after last month's steep price hikes triggered protests and forced the government to promise fresh relief at the pump.

Iran not our war says Tajani after Trump calls Europe irrelevant
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 12 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani on Friday stressed that, while the Iran war was not Europe's war, it has been doing what it could to bring about a solution, after President Donald Trump reportedly called the United States' European allies "irrelevant". Italian-American journalist Daniele Compatangelo told Italy's La7 television early on Friday that he had spoken to Trump on the telephone after he said an agreement to end the Iran war would be signed shortly and that Trump said "we won the war, their help was totally irrelevant" when asked about Italy,

items.title: Colosseum hosts 'most important exhibition on Troy ever held'
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 12 - The Colosseum is hosting the most important exhibition on Troy ever held, organisers said Friday saying the event will trace the voyage of Trojan hero Aeneas from the ruins of the Greek-torched ancient city in Asia Minor to Italy where he laid the groundwork for the foundation of the Eternal City. The show opening Friday and running till October 18 will include 300 ancient artifacts telling the story of Aeneas's legendary journey, and much more. "Troy and Rome. Myths, Legends, Stories of the Ancient Mediterranean" is part of a bilateral agreement signed between the Ital
